page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-24 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 48 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server provides 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-threaded performance, with Geekbench single-core scores and gzip compression rates placing in the top 25% tier. The server also demonstrates top-tier performance in LLM text generation for small 135M models, reaching 433.41 tokens per second. Multi-core operations, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth is measured at 140.86 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 57.78 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions, Advanced Matrix Extensions for tile and int8 operations, and cryptographic acceleration via VAES and SHA_NI. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is highly suited for tasks such as software compilation, image processing, text processing, and small-scale machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-24 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 48 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at 2.3 GHz with 12 physical cores and a 260 MB L3 cache. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core performance, particularly in text and image processing, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound tasks by minimizing memory overhead. It is well-suited for software compilation, text processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-24 is a compute-optimized instance with 24 vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering strong single-core and small LLM inference performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-24 features 24 vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for compute-heavy workloads without local storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for high-throughput CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated vCPUs and 48 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix math
Strong single-core performance in text processing and gzip compression benchmarks
Top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale LLM inference workloads
Optimized for compute-heavy tasks like software compilation and image processing
